events.title: Thousands of U.S. troops deploy to Middle East. And, the latest on DHS funding talks
events.summary: The U.S. is sending thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne to the Middle East. And, congressional Republicans present Democrats with a new deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security.

events.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions, state TV says
events.summary: Iran submitted a counterproposal that includes safeguards against future attacks against Iran and the country's right to "sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz," Iranian state Press TV reported.

events.title: Islandwide blackouts bring life in Cuba to a near total halt
events.summary: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.

events.title: US and China both believe the other is a declining power
events.summary: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.

events.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
events.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

events.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
events.summary: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

events.title: Will President Trump act on his threat to take Cuba?
events.summary: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.

events.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
events.summary: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

events.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
events.summary: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

events.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
events.summary: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.

events.title: Germany's big climate move — bold step or bare minimum?
events.summary: The German government has rolled out a multibillion‑euro climate package to turbocharge wind power and e‑mobility. Supporters call it overdue momentum, but critics say it barely scratches the surface.

events.title: How countries are tackling the global energy crisis
events.summary: With energy prices soaring and the Strait of Hormuz still closed, the world must cut demand. From 4-day workweeks and petrol rations to turning down the air con, here's what some countries are trying.

events.title: Iran war: Tehran says US is 'negotiating with itself'
events.summary: Iran has confirmed receipt of a US ceasefire plan conveyed via Pakistan but has rejected its proposals as "excessive." Tehran insists it is not negotiating with Washington as missile strikes continue. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Report: Volkswagen to switch from cars to defense at key plant
events.summary: A report in the Financial Times says Volkswagen is considering producing weapons for Israel. A VW spokesperson told DW the company was exploring "viable options."

events.title: North Korea holds lavish welcome for Belarus president
events.summary: A grand welcome in Pyongyang united North Korea's Kim Jong Un and Belarus' Alexander Lukashenko. The meeting comes as both leaders face continued criticism from the West, particularly for their close ties to Russia.

events.title: Nigeria moves to broaden security alliances amid ongoing insurgency threats
events.summary: Nigeria is expanding security partnerships as extremist threats rise, strengthening cooperation with France, receiving continued US military support, and renewing engagement with EU leaders.

events.title: Can Thailand's new government deliver on growth promises?
events.summary: After taking a gamble in seeking a new mandate, Thailand's newly reelected PM Anutin Charnvirakul has cast himself as a force for stability. But his government will quickly be tested by steep economic challenges.

events.title: Berlin court jails 4 men over Hamas weapon stockpiles
events.summary: A court in Berlin has ruled that four men gathered and stored weapons for use in terror attacks as members of a Hamas-linked cell. Prosecutors listed potential targets in Germany, including US and Israeli sites.

events.title: Is Iran cashing in millions from Strait of Hormuz blockade?
events.summary: An Iranian lawmaker claims Tehran is quietly charging tankers up to $2 million for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. The move threatens to turn the key waterway into a high-stakes toll booth.

events.title: From Goethe to Soraya: German-Iranian stories
events.summary: It's hard to imagine now, but Iran and Germany were once surprisingly close. A master poet, a German "Lawrence of Arabia," and an empress all played a role in this shared history.

events.title: Ukraine updates: Hungary threatens gas stop amid oil dispute
events.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban says Hungary will gradually stop the flow of gas to Ukraine until Russian Druzhba pipeline oil deliveries restart. Meanwhile, Russia reports widespread overnight drone strikes. DW has more.

events.title: Two arrested over London Jewish ambulance arson
events.summary: London police have arrested two men over the arson attack on Jewish community ambulances. CCTV suggests more involved.

events.title: Football: St. Pauli coach comes out as gay, criticizes game
events.summary: A youth coach at Bundesliga football club FC St. Pauli has publicly come out as gay and strongly criticized attitudes towards homosexuality in Germany's most popular sport.

events.title: Germany news: Race against time for stranded humpback whale
events.summary: Efforts to free a humpback whale trapped in shallow waters have so far failed, but wheels are in motion to free the creature. Meanwhile, police are guarding a nuclear waste convoy in western Germany. DW has the latest.

events.title: How well armed is Iran, and can it replenish missiles?
events.summary: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu say that Iran's missile capacity is "destroyed" and "degraded," yet Iran still strikes. How many missiles and drones remain, and how quickly can Iran rebuild its arsenal?

events.title: WNBA's labor deal is a structural shift for women's sports
events.summary: America's top women's basketball league just agreed to a new CBA. It will have a significant impact on women's sport.

events.title: Japan says Chinese Embassy break-in 'regrettable'
events.summary: A soldier breaking into the Chinese Embassy in Tokyo, reportedly with a knife, is making matters worse for diplomatic relations between the two countries. Ties were already stressed over the Taiwan issue.

events.title: Indians flock to fuel pumps as Iran war drives shortage fears
events.summary: From Gujarat to Tamil Nadu, long lines at fuel pumps reflect growing anxiety over oil supplies even as officials urge calm and assure of adequate supply. DW has the latest.

events.title: Meta hit with $375 million fine in child exploitation case
events.summary: While the New Mexico jury's landmark decision to hold Meta liable could be a bellwether, the penalty is only a fraction of what prosecutors sought.

events.title: Danish PM's left-wing bloc wins vote but with no majority
events.summary: It is unclear if Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen can secure a third term, as her party came in first but recorded a poor result. Foreign Minister Rasmussen became a kingmaker ahead of thorny negotiations.

events.title: French municipal vote: What do results mean for 2027 ballot?
events.summary: France's far-right National Rally did not do as well as it hoped in recent local elections. But, experts say, mainstream parties can't afford to relax ahead of the 2027 presidential elections.

events.title: NASA plans moon base instead of orbital lunar station
events.summary: New NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman says the US space agency will pivot from launching an orbiting lunar station to constructing a base on the moon's surface. It comes amid a renewed moon race with Russia and China.

events.title: Who wins what in the EU's new trade deal with Australia?
events.summary: Squeezed by Trump's tariffs and China's industrial muscle, the EU and Australia bury old trade disputes to finalize a long-delayed free trade deal.

events.title: Lithuanian PM: Stray crashed drone hailed from Ukraine
events.summary: Lithuania's prime minister has said that a military drone that crashed near the border to Belarus hailed from Ukraine and was aimed at Russian energy infrastructure. She said it was "part of a wider security picture."

events.title: American apocalypse: The end 'feels personal and imminent'
events.summary: Social psychologists in the US found that a third of people surveyed feel the world will end in their lifetimes. That's not only depressing; it also affects people's sense of agency when faced by war or climate change.

events.title: Germany's AfD: The new neoliberal workers' party?
events.summary: Workers in Germany are increasingly voting for the far-right, populist Alternative for Germany party. That is despite the fact the party's platform is not even particularly worker-friendly.

events.title: Anger in Cyprus as Iran war spotlights 'colonial' UK bases
events.summary: Cyprus wants a "frank discussion" about the future of UK bases. A drone incident in early March has blown open old debates across this de facto divided island.

events.title: Germany laments Trump's 'avoidable, unnecessary' Iran war
events.summary: Marking 75 years since the Foreign Ministry's post-war reformation, Germany's president and foreign minister warned of "profound change" in ties with Berlin's most important ally, the US, amid an "unnecessary" Iran war.

events.title: Australia and EU strike free trade deal
events.summary: The European Union and Australia have agreed on a sweeping new trade deal, compromising to boost exports in the face of global uncertainty over trade.

events.title: UK police see antisemitic motive in arson of ambulances
events.summary: British counterterrorism police are investigating an arson attack on ambulances operated by Jewish charity in north London.

events.title: Argentina's 'Dirty War' still on trial 50 years later
events.summary: Some 30,000 people were "disappeared" under the brutal military dictatorship that ruled Argentina from 1976 to 1983. Victims are still fighting for justice.

events.title: Ukraine nationals detained in India: Is Russia involved?
events.summary: Indian authorities accuse six Ukrainians and an American of supporting insurgent groups along the India-Myanmar border. Ukraine suggests that the case might be politically motivated.

events.title: EU reacts to 'concerning' report of Hungarian leak to Russia
events.summary: The European Union and Germany have expressed concern at reports of Hungary's foreign minister briefing Russia on EU talks. Prime Minister Viktor Orban has launched an investigation into what he's calling wiretapping.

events.title: Germany: Merz's government is running out of time
events.summary: The government in Berlin is under pressure to get major reforms underway. But Merz's coalition partner, the SPD, stands severely weakened following two defeats in state elections, and now finds it hard to compromise.

events.title: Report: Hungary using more Russian oil, despite EU phase out
events.summary: A new report by the Center for the Study of Democracy, shared exclusively with DW, says that Hungary has increased its dependence on Russian oil since 2021. It has also become highly dependent on Russian gas.

events.title: Italy's Meloni concedes defeat on judiciary referendum
events.summary: With Italian voters rejecting the judicial reform pushed by Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, the far-right leader lamented the "lost chance" to modernize the country.

events.title: Asian markets tumble, but Europe, US rally on Trump optimism
events.summary: Markets in Asia nosedived as trading restarted on Monday. Europe's indices were headed in the same direction, until optimistic Iran comments from Donald Trump prompted traders in Europe and the US to start buying again.

events.title: France: Former PM Lionel Jospin dies aged 88
events.summary: Jospin proved the canary in the coal mine for the far-right rise in France. He was the first center-left presidential candidate to finish behind Jean-Marie Le Pen but rallied his supporters to his rival, Jacques Chirac.

events.title: Iran's energy sites still on Trump's target list
events.summary: US President Trump has delayed strikes on Iran's power plants for five days, citing "productive" talks with Tehran. DW asks what infrastructure US forces could still bomb to force a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: How Africa gets shortchanged in trade with Europe
events.summary: The establishment of free trade between much of both continents for half a century has created growth in many areas. However, the data analysis shows that Africa remains on unequal footing.

events.title: Iranians and Israelis united through music
events.summary: The Berlin-based music ensemble Sistanagila brings together musicians from Iran and Israel who offer a message of hope for a better future.

events.title: Alexandra Popp and Borussia Dortmund: A sign of the times
events.summary: Germany's most famous female footballer has signed for a third-division team. The transfer will have huge impacts across the women's game in Germany.

events.title: Ilya Remeslo, formerly a pro-Putin blogger, has ended up in a Russian psychiatric hospital after criticizing the president. What happened?
events.summary: After seemingly switching sides, a pro-government blogger in Russia who criticized President Vladimir Putin has been admitted to psychiatric care in St. Petersburg. Did he go rogue?

events.title: What's next for Germany's troubled car giants?
events.summary: German automakers are in a crisis due to stagnant sales, falling profits and constant strategy shifts. Can the industry recover?

events.title: Earth's climate more unbalanced than ever, WMO warns
events.summary: The world's oceans have broken heat records for nine straight years, glaciers are retreating and extreme weather is killing thousands. The only way to avoid the worst is to urgently ditch fossil fuels.

events.title: Slovenia election sees ruling party tied with opposition
events.summary: Prime Minister Robert Golob's governing liberal Freedom Movement of Slovenia was almost perfectly tied with Janez Jansa and his right-wing SDS party, with 99% of votes counted.

events.title: France: Leftist Emmanuel Gregoire becomes new mayor of Paris
events.summary: Gregoire replaces fellow socialist Anne Hidalgo as the French capital’s mayor. The local elections are being closely watched to gauge the political mood in the country ahead of next year’s presidential race.

events.title: Germany launches alliance to fairly recruit skilled workers
events.summary: Germany needs new talent for its labor market — and fresh ideas to ensure that foreign workers not only come, but also stay. New initiatives are now emerging to help businesses and skilled workers.

events.title: Amazonia's Indigenous peoples dismantle Western cliches
events.summary: Indigenous peoples and the Amazon rainforest have often been exoticized. A new exhibition challenges these long-held tropes.

events.title: Navigation satellites guide the world — and its wars
events.summary: From smartphone maps to precision weapons, satellite navigation underpins modern life and war. Global navigation satellite systems have become indispensable, and that's made them vulnerable to disruption and attack.

events.title: At 97, Japan's art icon Yayoi Kusama is Instagram-ready
events.summary: Despite her age, Yayoi Kusama's art and its subject matter remain highly relevant. A retrospective of the Japanese artist's work is now on show at Museum Ludwig in Cologne.

events.title: Germany news: CDU wins Rhineland-Palatinate election
events.summary: Provisional results show that Friedrich Merz's conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) has won the elections in Rhineland-Palatinate in western Germany.

events.title: Thessaloniki: Remembering the 'Jerusalem of the Balkans'
events.summary: The near-total annihilation of Greece’s Jewish communities sends a strong message to this day. Memorials, education and a new Holocaust museum aim to combat a rise in antisemitism and dangerous misconceptions.

events.title: Mass Prague rally hits Babis over democracy concerns
events.summary: Demonstrators accused Prime Minister Andrej Babis of democratic backsliding, citing shifts in the country's foreign and domestic policies toward what the call more pro-Russia positions.

events.title: Using every drop: Physics answers a crucial kitchen question
events.summary: How long does it take for the last drop of milk or oil to drip out of the bottle? US scientists have probed this everyday conundrum.

events.title: Taliban install new diplomat without telling Germany: Report
events.summary: Despite not being recognized by Germany, the Taliban have appointed a new head of the Afghan embassy in Berlin without the German government's approval, broadcaster ARD reported.

events.title: Germany's governing CDU, SPD watch latest regional election
events.summary: Voters are set to go to the polls in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany's wine-growing region and home of Donald Trump's ancestors. It's another test case for Germany's coalition government.

events.title: Czechs plan major rally as MPs mull 'foreign agent' law
events.summary: Prague could see one of its largest demonstrations in recent years on Saturday. Many Czechs are preparing to protest what organizers of the rally describe as democratic backsliding under Prime Minister Andrej Babis.

events.title: An answer to US drought conditions may be in the toilet
events.summary: Americans are willing to fork out extra money each month to drink recycled wastewater. With climate change intensifying drought in several regions, cities are exploring ways to turn sewage into drinking water.

events.title: Germany's finance minister rejects money-misuse accusations
events.summary: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil has begun to make use of billions in new debt intended to help revive Germany's economy. Is he spending this extraordinary sum appropriately? There are serious doubts.

events.title: Racism in Germany widespread, but more subtle than before
events.summary: Racism and discrimination are pervasive in Germany. Xenophobic and derogatory views remain rampant. To coincide with the International Day Against Racism, new studies have been presented.

events.title: Salman Rushdie on why tyrants fear artists
events.summary: As the renowned writer tours Germany to discuss his latest book, "The Eleventh Hour," he explains why he chooses to remain optimistic despite all of the world's reasons to despair.

events.title: Norwegian princess says she was 'manipulated' by Epstein
events.summary: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit gave an interview on her ties to the late US sex offender after new details came to light in the "Epstein files."

events.title: Slovenia election a battle for country's 'identity'
events.summary: The parliamentary election in Slovenia is shaping up to be a tight race between the governing center-left GS and the right-wing SDS. Nevertheless, smaller parties are likely to hold the key to forming a government.

events.title: BTS: Everything you need to know about the K-pop comeback of the year
events.summary: After a four-year hiatus and the end of their military service, BTS are returning as a full group. With a new album, a Netflix livestream and a major world tour on the horizon, the K-pop icons are entering a new chapter.

events.title: Drone defense in the Iran war: What can Ukraine offer?
events.summary: As the Iran war continues apace, Ukraine's hard-won expertise in countering drone swarms is sparking interest around the world.

events.title: Nuclear's cleanup cost threatens the expansion dream
events.summary: Countries are racing to build new reactors. But we've barely figured out how to clean up the old ones — and the bill is potentially staggering.

events.title: Iran war: Why is the South Pars gas field so important?
events.summary: An attack on Iran's key South Pars gas field has rattled global energy markets, raised fears of further escalation and driven oil prices even higher.

events.title: Bayern Munich's Karl and Urbig in latest Germany squad
events.summary: Julian Nagelsmann's latest Germany squad includes a host of new names. The 2026 World Cup is less than three months away.

events.title: The people remodelling homes with reclaimed ruins
events.summary: Cities are quietly becoming raw‑material hubs as urban miners add rubble to the carbon‑saving construction supply chain.

events.title: 'Value of African football will go down' after AFCON final u-turn
events.summary: After Senegal were stripped of their title and Morocco were crowned winners, African football faces some big questions.

events.title: Iran war triggers helium shortage, hits semiconductor supply
events.summary: Rising tension in the Middle East is rattling high-end chipmakers. Among the latest geopolitical uncertainties for the industry is a shortage of helium that could slow global production.

events.title: The internet was supposed to be free. What went wrong?
events.summary: Thirty years ago, countercultural thinkers declared the internet an independent space. Today, it's controlled by a handful of corporations. Were we deceived?

events.title: No more big spenders: Iran war to dent Gulf state investment
events.summary: Gulf states like Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the UAE have invested, or promised to invest, trillions around the world, everywhere from America to Africa. The Iran war may see their money spent closer to home.

events.title: The German village running on its own juice
events.summary: While war in the Middle East has sent oil prices soaring and households bracing for higher bills, one tiny German village has spent 30 years making itself immune to exactly this kind of shock.

events.title: Ancient graffiti reveals scenes of everyday life in Pompeii
events.summary: A growing number of researchers are turning to Pompeii's graffiti to better understand the lives, humor and concerns of ordinary people nearly 2,000 years ago. Spoiler alert: They liked gladiators.

events.title: What does the Iran war mean for the US defense sector?
events.summary: US defense companies are being called on to ramp up production to meet fresh demand from the war in Iran. It opens up an existing rift between them and the Trump administration.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Is the US-Iran war an ethical tipping point?
events.summary: The US is the main host of the 2026 World Cup. But it's currently at war with Iran, which has qualified for the tournament. The ethical conflicts of this soccer competition grow more complex each week.

events.title: Germany news: Strike to block all flights at Berlin airport
events.summary: Berlin-Brandenburg Airport is to ground all flights amid a strike called for Wednesday. Meanwhile, Chancellor Friedrich Merz's CDU is set to remain the strongest party at the local level in the state of Hesse. Follow DW.

events.title: Iran war: Germany's Merz distances himself from Trump
events.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has changed his tune and voiced criticism of the US President Donald Trump and his war on Iran. After having long shown understanding, he now stresses that Germany will not get involved.

events.title: Iran war: Why gold prices are not soaring
events.summary: Gold is typically seen as a safe haven, especially in times of war and other crises. Notwithstanding the ongoing war in Iran, the price is steady. What's driving the stability?

events.title: Germany's Climate Protection measures are barely on target
events.summary: Germany has managed to meet its climate target for 2025. But only just. Environment Minister Schneider is calling for greater efforts, yet there are signs of a rift within the government.

events.title: Can Trump sell the MAGA crowd on soccer with the World Cup?
events.summary: Donald Trump has mobilized around right-wing coded sports like MMA and wrestling, but his soccer World Cup push is a cultural clash.

events.title: Oscars 2026: 'One Battle After Another' wins Best Picture
events.summary: "Frankenstein" and "Sinners" were the other big winners of Hollywood's biggest night, with Michael B. Jordan taking the Best Actor Award and Jessie Buckley Best Actress.

events.title: German Catholic Church consecrates first bishop from India
events.summary: Religious brother Joshy George Pottackal, born in India, is to become the first auxiliary bishop of non-European heritage. The diversity of the global Catholic Church is reaching the German episcopate.

events.title: Instagram accounts trivialize the Holocaust
events.summary: In Instagram posts, Nazi perpetrators are glorified, while their war crimes and role in the Holocaust are omitted. Victims' organizations call it an "attack on the dignity of the survivors."

events.title: The BKA, 'Germany's FBI', turns 75
events.summary: Founded in 1951, the Federal Criminal Police Office (BKA) was dominated for decades by men with Nazi pasts. Today, the BKA is a central pillar of Germany's security architecture — even on the international stage.

events.title: Celebrated philosopher Jürgen Habermas dies aged 96
events.summary: One of Germany's greatest postwar intellectuals, he continuously argued for a robust and inclusive democracy. His passing marks the end of an era.

events.title: Germany's Greens: More than leftist, woke ecologists?
events.summary: Cem Özdemir won the election in Baden-Württemberg partly by abandoning many of his Green Party's core issues. His party is still debating whether this was a good move.

events.title: US-Israeli strikes damage Iran's cultural heritage sites
events.summary: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are at risk, with several monuments, including palaces and a historic mosque, already damaged by US and Israeli strikes, which began on February 28.

events.title: US eases oil sanctions at an ideal time for Russia
events.summary: The United States has further eased sanctions on oil from Russia in an attempt to bring energy prices down as strikes on Iran continue. The news is a huge boost to the Kremlin, just when it needed one.

events.title: Who holds the biggest emergency oil reserves?
events.summary: Iran's effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has pushed dozens of countries to tap their emergency oil stockpiles. But what exactly are strategic reserves, and who holds the world's biggest crisis-ready supplies?

events.title: White hydrogen: The hidden gas that could transform energy
events.summary: Trillions of tons of hydrogen lie in Earth's crust. Can a Bavarian geologist unlock this clean and cheap energy source?

events.title: Dealing with annoying people might make you age faster
events.summary: Stressful people in your life are linked to faster biological aging, a US study finds. Forced proximity — especially with family — appears to worsen the effect.

events.title: Who will win the Oscars?
events.summary: Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" and Paul Thomas Anderson's "One Battle After Another" are favorites in top categories, while Timothee Chalamet's viral moment has rocked awards season.

events.title: Fritz Lang's 'Metropolis': The future is now
events.summary: Lang's classic 1927 silent film, which was set in 2026, predicted a world that now looks eerily familiar, with a robot hinting at the future direction of AI.

events.title: Iran war: Strait of Hormuz shutdown could spark food crisis
events.summary: Along with spiking oil prices, Iran's de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz is raising fertilizer costs by up to a third. Farmers are bracing for soil nutrient shortages that threaten lower harvests.

events.title: Iran war risks long-term toxic legacy for people and nature that ripples beyond borders
events.summary: The fallout from fighting in the Middle East, from burning oil refineries to sunken ships, threatens lasting repercussions for the health of the region's people, water and food supplies, and ecosystems.

events.title: AI: Could Germany adopt Anthropic?
events.summary: After Donald Trump ordered the US government to cancel all contracts with Anthropic, a German politician has said Germany should offer to bring the AI firm to Europe. Is that viable?

events.title: Germany: 7-year-old flashes €5,000 cash at Osnabrück school
events.summary: The youngster found a cash-filled envelope at home that his parents had withdrawn to buy a car, but not before he took it to class for an extra-curricular "if I were a rich man" lesson with his friends.

events.title: How oil price hikes threaten Germany's economy
events.summary: How high will the Iran war drive up the prices of oil, gasoline and diesel? There is great concern that the price shock could dash hopes for economic recovery in Germany.

events.title: Honda warns of $16bn hit on its pivot away from EVs
events.summary: Japan's Honda expects to book extra costs of almost $16 billion as it recalibrates its electric vehicle strategy. It blamed US policy changes and tariffs in part, as well as reduced competitiveness in Asia.

events.title: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives
events.summary: A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars.

events.title: WAFCON 2026: A 'combination of factors' behind postponement
events.summary: African football's governing body CAF says it made the decision to postpone it's top women's tournament to ensure "the best possible conditions." It's not the first time the tournament has been moved at short notice.

events.title: Germany news: Lufthansa pilots stage another strike
events.summary: A two-day strike by Lufthansa pilots is expected to lead to many cancellations. The German chancellor is visiting Norway, a key energy supplier to his country.

events.title: How Poland is flexing its economic muscle in Western Europe
events.summary: Poland's economy is now a European star performer. A new trend suggests further momentum, with more and more Polish firms acquiring foreign companies, including in Germany.

events.title: Trump's arch plan and the history of gateways
events.summary: The proposed 250‑foot arch has sparked debate in Washington. But monumental gateways have shaped politics, memory, and ritual across cultures for millennia.

events.title: Merz says Germany won't join EU return to nuclear energy
events.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is in favor of a proposal to build new nuclear power plants in the EU. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says it's impossible.

events.title: Germany, others partially release oil reserves amid Iran war
events.summary: Germany, Austria, Japan, Spain and France have all indicated plans to release some of their oil reserves, a day after the International Energy Agency was debating the pros and cons. The Iran war has driven prices up.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Jordan fans frustrated by US visa hurdles
events.summary: Jordan is set to make its World Cup debut, but military conflict and the US government may keep fans at home. Those wishing to travel with their team are calling for answers on visas from FIFA and the US.

events.title: Iran war roils oil trade, casting doubt on US fossil fuel push
events.summary: The Iran war has sent oil and gas prices surging. As countries like the US double down on fossil fuels, experts say the conflict shows how speeding up homegrown renewables is the only way to avoid future energy crises.

events.title: Volkswagen Group profits take big hit on Porsche shift
events.summary: Strategic readjustment at Porsche, as well as dwindling sales in both main export markets, the US and China took a big bite out of VW's 2025 profits — which almost halved.

events.title: Iran war: How long before Gulf nations stop pumping oil?
events.summary: The oil price spiked near $120 after strikes on Iran's energy sites and the shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. With tankers stuck and Gulf oil facilities hit, producers are running short on storage to maintain production.

events.title: AI lab Anthropic sues to block Pentagon blacklisting
events.summary: Tech start-up Anthropic has refused to meet the Pentagon's demands for unrestricted military use of AI, setting up a legal showdown with the Trump administration.

events.title: Fears grow for Iran's women's football team
events.summary: Calls are growing for Australia to offer asylum to the Iranian national team at the Asian Cup. With serious punishment looming, five players have reportedly escaped Iran's supervision amid safety concerns.

events.title: Long before AI, fake photos were already popular
events.summary: From anti-Nazi photo montages to staged photos, an exhibition in Amsterdam focuses on the long history of manipulated visuals.

events.title: Plasticizer chemical levels in German children raise concern
events.summary: Tests in Germany show children and teens are contaminated with banned plasticizers. You may never have heard of these potentially toxic chemicals, but they're everywhere, including clothes, cosmetics and food packaging.

events.title: One in three Gen Z men want obedient women
events.summary: Many young men embrace deeply conservative gender roles, a new international study finds. Is social media stoking the return of traditionalism?

events.title: International Women's Day: Workplace equality needs action
events.summary: Women in Germany who wish to have careers and families face systemic challenges. The German sociologist Jutta Allmendinger sees reason for optimism, with countries like Iceland showing the way.

events.title: Why all the hype for 'The Devil Wears Prada 2'?
events.summary: Fans are eagerly awaiting the sequel ― and the return of those iconic characters and their vicious power plays. But "The Devil Wears Prada 2" also shows us just how much fashion and media have changed since 2006.

events.title: Formula 1 in 2026: Just Formula E on steroids?
events.summary: Formula 1 is kicking off the new season with some big rule changes. Drivers will have to focus on energy management—and not everyone is thrilled about it. Plus, there are two new teams: Audi and Cadillac.

events.title: Ecosystem collapse could fuel next global security crisis
events.summary: Debt-for-nature swaps and conservation funds to halt biodiversity loss are gaining traction as governments link ecosystem collapse to geopolitical instability.

events.title: Michelangelo: The man, the brand, the mystery
events.summary: The Renaissance artist's name recently made headlines as claims of a secret deathbed trove of artworks and a rediscovered bust surfaced. But what do we actually know about his life?

events.title: F1's Laura Müller continuing to blaze a trail for women
events.summary: Laura Müller is Formula 1's first female race engineer, taking crucial decisions for Haas driver Esteban Ocon. On International Women's Day, Turn 6 at Melbourne's Albert Park race track is to be named after her.

events.title: India news: India reach T20 final in shootout with England
events.summary: India's 253 runs in Mumbai proved just enough to best England and reach the cricket T20 World Cup final. Meanwhile, Finland's Alexander Stubb is in Delhi and polls have closed in neighboring Nepal.

events.title: Football: Women's AFCON postponed at 11th hour
events.summary: After weeks of uncertainty, football's governing body in Africa has announced the postponement of the Women's Africa Cup of Nations, which had been slated to start in mid-March.

events.title: Paralympic Winter Games: What you need to know
events.summary: Just weeks after hosting the Winter Olympics, northern Italy is set to host the Winter Paralympic Games. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

events.title: Salute replaces silence for Iran after 'message from home'
events.summary: In a change from the silence of their first game, Iran's women's national team saluted their anthem against Australia on Thursday. It appears to be another case of authorities cracking down on sport as a tool of protest.

events.title: Tricia Tuttle to remain Berlinale head — with new code of conduct
events.summary: Facing political backlash following Pro-Palestinian speeches during the festival's closing gala, Tuttle received widespread support from the international film industry.

events.title: Why it's worth getting a heat pump if you live in a cold country, too
events.summary: Worldwide, people are increasingly turning to heat pumps to warm their home. The devices save money, and they're good for the environment. In Northern Europe, they've long proved their worth in colder climates.

events.title: World Cup 2026: US-Iran war adds to list of concerns
events.summary: With 100 days to go until World Cup 2026 starts, the war between co-host USA and qualifiers Iran poses serious questions. Spiraling prices, safety and reluctant host cities are among the other unresolved issues.

events.title: How sport is being disrupted by the US-Israel war with Iran
events.summary: The war against Iran launched by the United States and Israel — and Iran's retaliation — have had quite an impact on sports. DW looks at some of the conflict's current effects, and at what could be ahead.

events.title: Vincent Kompany: Bayern Munich's coach with a cause
events.summary: Vincent Kompany is preparing for a Bundesliga title fight. But the Belgian has also been speaking out after the racism suffered by Real Madrid’s Vinicius Jnr, a cause that has been formative in his life and career.

events.title: New HIV drug may end multi-pill regimen for older people
events.summary: People who have lived with HIV long-term can need up to a dozen pills every day to suppress the virus. Results from a new drug trial raise hope of changing that.

events.title: Europe bags savings as America chases fossil‑fuel nostalgia
events.summary: While Washington talks up fossil fuels, EU countries are cashing in on efficiency and low‑cost renewables.

events.title: The DIY solar hack arriving in US homes
events.summary: Americans are embracing easy, plug‑and‑play solar units that slash energy costs — even as Washington tries to slow the clean‑energy shift.

events.title: Football: Afghanistan women again set to fight for future
events.summary: Afghanistan's female players are used to fighting and waiting. Months after their reintroduction to internationals, they now wait on another game and must wrestle with FIFA doubling down on a country that rejected them.

events.title: Clashes spark safety concerns for World Cup in Mexico
events.summary: A wave of violence set off by the killing of a Mexican drug cartel boss has raised concerns about security ahead of the World Cup. FIFA and Mexican offiicals have played down any risk.

events.title: NATO officer says allies must urgently rethink defence, prepare for 'new enemy'
events.summary: A top NATO official on Wednesday urged allied countries to rethink how they ensure their defence. Pierre Vandier told a security and defence forum in Paris that many countries have yet to fully grasp the scale of the challenge, particularly when it comes to boosting weapons production, adding that it was not a question of money but of speed.

events.title: Where do French parties stand a year before the presidential election?
events.summary: With local elections done and dusted, France is pivoting towards electing a president to replace Emmanuel Macron in 2027. The local elections have revealed that the political landscape is fragmented, and that party alliances have reshaped. On the left, the socialist party is in turmoil over an alliance with the radical left party France Unbowed. On the right, the question is whether to move toward the centre of further shift right, towards the far-right party National Rally. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali looks at where parties stand a year from the election.

events.title: Ex-EU border chief faces French investigation for crimes against humanity
events.summary: The Paris Court of Appeal opened an investigation into the former head of the EU Border agency on March 24 for potentially participating in crimes against humanity. The former border chief, Fabrice Leggeri resigned in 2022 following allegations of mistreatment of migrants at EU borders. Now a lawmaker for France's far-right National Rally party, he denies the allegations.

events.title: Denmark faces tough coalition talks following election
events.summary: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen handed in her government's resignation. The move comes after her three-party coalition suffered a large defeat in March 25's snap elections. Frederiksen faces tough coalition talks if she wants to remain PM. Since neither the left nor right leaning blocs won a majority in Denmark, Frederiksen's fate likely lies is in the hands of the centrist Moderate party of Lars Lugge Rasmussen, a foreign minister in her own government.

events.title: Heritage sites in the Middle East under threat due to conflict
events.summary: The Middle East war is having an impact on the region's heritage sites. Archaeological and Unesco heritage sites have been collateral damage in the war, on all sides. Unesco believes at least 6 heritage sites in the Middle East have been damaged, the Tyre site in Lebanon, four in Iran and the White City in Israel. In Iran, the historic city of isfahan and the Golestan palace in Tehran were also damaged.

events.title: Iran 'dismisses' US 15-point peace plan, issues its own ceasefire proposal
events.summary: Iran on March 25 an American plan to pause the war in the Middle East, issuing its own counterproposal instead, as it launched more attacks on Israel and Gulf Arab countries. Its own plan includes a halt to killings of its officials, means to make sure no other war is waged against it, reparations for the war, the end of hostilities and Iran’s “exercise of sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.” FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look.

events.title: Emblematic MeToo trial of Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan awaits verdict
events.summary: Tariq Ramadan, a well-known Swiss-Egyptian Islamic scholar, has been tried in absentia for the rape of three women in Paris, with the verdict to fall on March 25. The trial is one of the most emblematic of the MeToo movement in France. He has already been convicted in Switzerland, where he is currently hospitalised for multiple sclerosis - which his medical team judged made him unable to attend his trial in France. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley looks at the details of the trial, and who the accused is.

events.title: Cancer nurse turned archbishop installed as first female Church of England leader
events.summary: A former cancer nurse who became a priest at the age of 40 is to become archbishop of Canterbury, publicly celebrating her election as the first woman to lead the Church of England. Although she was nominated in January, the ceremony marks the symbolic start of her public ministry. Her appointment has been criticised by conservative voices in the Church, who continue to oppose the possibility for women to be priests at all. FRANCE 24's Bénédicte Paviot breaks it down for us.

events.title: Ukrainian drones crash in Estonia and Latvia, raising air defence concerns
events.summary: Stray Ukrainian drones hit Estonia and Latvia early on March 25, one of them slamming into a chimney at a power station. There were no reports of injuries in the incidents, and the drones were believed to be part of a wider Ukrainian attack on Russia targeting energy infrastructure. Latvia says these drone incursions could keep happening in the Baltic region.

events.title: Once seeking de-escalation, Saudi Arabia now sees ‘no coexistence’ with hardline Iran
events.summary: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Natasha Lindstaedt, Author, Professor of Government at the University of Essex. The unfolding conflict reveals a profound shift in Gulf strategic thinking, Professor Lindstaedt argues, driven less by ideological ambition than by accumulated insecurity. What began as a strong preference for de-escalation has gradually given way to a more hard-edged assessment of regional realities. The persistence of attacks, particularly on critical infrastructure, has reinforced the perception that coexistence with a hardline Iranian regime may no longer be viable.

events.title: French population overexposed to cadmium through food, health agency says
events.summary: The French population is overexposed to cadmium through food sources, according to a comprehensive report published Wednesday by the national environmental health agency. Cadmium is a toxic metal that is ubiquitous in the environment and can contaminate soil, water and air.

events.title: US goals shift: 'At the beginning of the Iran war, the US and Israel were on the same page'
events.summary: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Dr H. A. Hellyer, Senior Associate Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) for Defence and International Security Studies in London. He examines the evolving dynamics between Washington and Tel Aviv in the context of the war on Iran, alongside Israel’s expanding military posture in Lebanon. The central observation is that while initial strategic alignment existed, divergence has emerged, not necessarily in ultimate objectives, but in coherence, messaging, and political intent.

events.title: Film show: 'Anemone', a Day-Lewis family production
events.summary: The only person who managed to coax Daniel Day-Lewis out of his eight-year retirement was a certain Ronan Day-Lewis, whose début feature "Anemone" stars his father. Film critic Emma Jones tells us why a mesmerising Day-Lewis senior steals the show with the intensity of his performance.

events.title: US demands of 15-point peace plan likely 'unacceptable' for Iran
events.summary: The US announced it had sent on a 15-point peace plan to Iran, which Tehran has deemed 'excessive'. But what is the US's list of demands? There are some indications: sanction relief, a rollback of Iran's nuclear programme, the monitoring by the IAEA of that programme, shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and an end to support for proxies. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look at the latest developments.

events.title: In Nairobi, residents blame flood devastation on inadequate infrastructure
events.summary: At least 21 Kenyan counties have been affected by floods since their start in March, raising concerns over public safety, infrastructure damage and growing humanitarian needs. According to authorities, over 34,000 people have been displaced. The capital, Nairobi, has been particularly hit. Although the governor has urgently launched operations to clear drainage systems, residents say the severe flooding is partly due to a city that is growing too quickly. FRANCE 24's team went to Nairobi to learn more.

events.title: Ghana and EU sign defence partnership to counter west Africa insecurity
events.summary: Ghana and the European Union on March 25 signed their first formal defence partnership. It has also signed a similar agreement with Iceland and Australia. It comes as Ghana tries to prevent a spillover of increased attacks from groups backed by Al-Qaeda and the Islamic State in the region. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo tells us what the partnership entails, from Accra.

events.title: Ukraine: Massive wave of daytime Russian strikes hits sites nationwide
events.summary:
In one of the largest daytime air strikes since the start of the war, #Russia launched nearly 1,000 drones at various sites in #Ukraine on March 24.
 
The raids claimed several lives and caused damage in various cities, hundreds of kilometres from the front line.

events.title: Danish election signals shift toward Dutch and Belgian-style of coalition building
events.summary: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Ulrik Pram Gad, Senior Researcher at the Danish Institute for International Studies. He observes a political landscape marked less by decisive outcomes and more by fragmentation and negotiation. Electoral erosion among traditional parties continues, yet not uniformly, leaving space for incumbency without dominance. Strategic timing, shaped by temporary surges in approval, has not reversed longer-term structural trends. External crises have influenced perception but have not singularly determined voter behaviour.

events.title: France overexposed to heavy metal cadmium due to food
events.summary: The French Agency for Food, Environmental and Occupational Health & Safety has released alarming call to the government to lower the levels of cadmium found in food. Compared to other EU nations, cadmium levels in France are two to three times higher. Not only that, but level shave nearly doubled in the past decade, with over 47% of people having dangerous quantities of cadmium in their bodies.

events.title: Famed Lille bakery shares story behind its 'gaufres' on International Waffle Day
events.summary: This Wednesday is International Waffle Day – a celebration for waffle lovers everywhere, but particularly here in France, where les gaufres are a particular fan favourite. We're focusing on a very particular kind of waffle, one that's not big and puffy like the ones you might find in Belgium. These waffles come from the northern French town of Lille and the bakery Meert. Gregori Pillegand, Meert's international development director, tells us what's so special about their kind of gaufre.

events.title: Suspicious bets placed on US-Iran strikes trigger insider trading concerns

It's raising questions on if anyone acted on insider knowledge.

events.title: New Mexico trial finds Meta chose 'growth and profit over children's safety'

The trial is the first of several to be held in 2026 regarding social media child safety. The New Mexico jury found that the tech giant knowingly misled users when it came to child safety, and chose 'growth and profit' first, as FRANCE 24's Wassim Cornet explains from Los Angeles.

events.title: Oil supply shock hardens energy transition resolve – for some

events.title: 'Make cheese not war': How France's Larzac protests inspired demos around the world
events.summary: The author of a new book has spoken to FRANCE 24 about how the Larzac protests in France from 1971 became a modern-day template for demonstrations across the world. The protests began after the French government announced a huge extension of a military base on the Larzac plateau, referring to the farmers living there as "a few peasants" in "French Siberia". This sparked much anger, and residents ended up swearing oaths never to be forced from their land, pioneering regional radicalism for years to come. The author of the book "Make cheese not war. Transnational resistance and the Larzac in modern France" is Andrew Smith. He spoke to us in Perspective.
